title: Incorrect result for comparison of large number
description:
mrigger added on 2019-08-30 20:28:49:
Consider the following test case:
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 REAL UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES (3175546974276630385);
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE 3175546974276630385 < t0.c0; -- unexpected: row is not fetched
I would expect the row to be fetched, since the WHERE expression should evaluate to TRUE:
SELECT 3175546974276630385 < t0.c0 FROM t0; -- 1
However, it seems that different rounding is performed (at least on my machine). When removing the UNIQUE from the column, the comparison works as expected.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c1 DOUBLE PRECISION UNIQUE, c2 INTEGER);
INSERT INTO t1(c1, c2) VALUES (3175546974276630385::DOUBLE PRECISION, 1);
CREATE TABLE t2(c3 DOUBLE PRECISION, c4 TEXT);
INSERT INTO t2(c3, c4) VALUES (3175546974276630385::DOUBLE PRECISION, 'test');
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE 3175546974276630385::DOUBLE PRECISION < (SELECT MAX(c3) FROM t2);
key_bug_features: Comparison of large REAL numbers with UNIQUE constraint
